title: paraserianthes falcataria subsp. fulva
intro: Paraserianthes falcataria subsp. fulva, commonly known as the Burma teak or fulvous tawa, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Paraserianthes falcataria subsp. fulva include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, or extracts, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
